reason: Response B uses a single accumulating run-on sentence packed with concrete, escalating comic details (diluted-then-reconstituted ideas, pretend note-taking, a follow-up meeting to discuss the first), which fits Haiku's conversational, detail-driven humor. Response A reads as a model imitating wryness: tightly compressed aphorisms culminating in a polished quotable maxim ("Nothing solves itself by adding itself"), which feels more deliberately essayistic than genuine Haiku output. The specificity and momentum of B over the abstract epigram style of A points to B being the authentic response.
